hotspots.reason: Indirect indexing xv[cur_inds[j]] causes random memory access

bottleneck_type.reasoning: Irregular access pattern through cur_inds causes poor cache utilization. Only 2 FLOPs per memory access (multiply-add), very low arithmetic intensity (~0.25 FLOPs/byte).

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Matrix reordering (RCM, Metis) to improve locality
optimization_suggestions.details: Reorder rows/columns to cluster non-zeros near diagonal
Verify #pragma omp parallel for on outer i-loop is utilized effectively

gpu_suitability.reasoning: Many independent rows can be processed in parallel, but irregular access remains a challenge
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use cuSPARSE or implement with one thread per row, consider CSR5 or SELL-C-sigma formats
